name: azure-appconfiguration-ts
description: Build applications using Azure App Configuration SDK for JavaScript (@azure/app-configuration). Use when working with configuration settings, feature flags, Key Vault references, dynamic refresh, or centralized configuration management.
capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete configuration settings with labels
Implement feature flags with user targeting and group rollout
Enable dynamic configuration refresh without application restart
Integrate Azure Key Vault secrets with automatic refresh
Create and manage configuration snapshots for releases
Filter settings by key prefix, label, and tags
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation file (SKILL.md) containing reference examples for Azure App Configuration SDK. All 55 static analysis findings are false positives - the analyzer incorrectly treated Markdown code block delimiters and documentation examples as executable code. No actual security risks exist.
valueStatement: Manage application settings and feature flags centrally without redeployment. Azure App Configuration provides dynamic refresh and Key Vault integration for secure cloud-native applications.
userTitle: Build Apps with Azure App Configuration SDK
limitations: Requires Azure App Configuration resource and authentication setup
Feature flag targeting requires Microsoft.Targeting filter configuration
Dynamic refresh polling interval affects latency of configuration changes
Key Vault references require additional RBAC permissions for secret access

useCases.title: Cloud-Native Application Configuration
useCases.description: Centralize configuration for microservices across environments using labels. Enable dynamic updates without redeployment.

useCases.targetUser: Product managers and DevOps engineers managing feature releases
useCases.title: Feature Flag Rollout
useCases.description: Gradually release features to user groups with targeting conditions. A/B test functionality with controlled rollout percentages.

useCases.title: Secure Secret Management
useCases.description: Reference Key Vault secrets in configuration. Separate secrets from config while maintaining unified access patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Configuration Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up Azure App Configuration Client in TypeScript using DefaultAzureCredential. Include environment variable setup for the endpoint.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner setting up their first App Configuration integration

promptTemplates.title: Feature Flag Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a feature flag called 'NewCheckout' with user targeting. Show how to check if the flag is enabled for a specific user with userId and groups context.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer implementing feature flags for gradual rollout

promptTemplates.title: Dynamic Refresh Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up App Configuration Provider with automatic refresh every 30 seconds. Include Express middleware that triggers refresh on each request and event listeners for refresh notifications.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced user building real-time configuration updates

promptTemplates.title: Key Vault Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure App Configuration to resolve Key Vault references automatically. Set up secret refresh interval and show how to access resolved secrets in the application.

bestPractices: Use app-configuration-provider package for runtime configuration with automatic refresh support
Separate environments using labels (development, staging, production) for clean configuration management
Enable refresh with sentinel key pattern to trigger full configuration updates efficiently
antiPatterns: Using low-level SDK for application runtime configuration instead of provider package
Hardcoding connection strings in source code instead of using managed identity
Disabling refresh in production environments requiring frequent configuration updates

faq.question: What is the difference between App Configuration SDK and Provider?
faq.answer: The SDK (@azure/app-configuration) provides low-level CRUD operations for managing settings. The Provider (@azure/app-configuration-provider) is recommended for applications as it adds dynamic refresh, feature flags, and Key Vault integration.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure App Configuration?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential with the App Configuration endpoint for managed identity authentication. Alternatively, use connection strings with endpoint, ID, and secret for development scenarios.

faq.question: Can I use App Configuration for secrets management?
faq.answer: Yes, use Key Vault references. Store secrets in Key Vault and reference them in App Configuration. Secrets are automatically resolved and refreshed based on the configured interval.

faq.question: How does dynamic refresh work?
faq.answer: The provider polls App Configuration at configured intervals. When a sentinel key changes, it triggers a full refresh. Refresh is non-blocking and emits events for notification.

faq.question: What are feature flag filters and how do I use them?
faq.answer: Filters determine flag evaluation. Microsoft.Targeting filter supports user IDs, groups with rollout percentages, and default rollout. Configure in the flag's conditions.clientFilters array.

faq.question: How do I rollback configuration changes?
faq.answer: Use snapshots to capture configuration state before releases. Archive and recover snapshots as needed. Load configuration from a specific snapshot using snapshotName in selectors.
